ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for mentaiko-sauced crunchy mushroom specifically; closest parent 'mushroom food product' (FOODON_03301710) used as anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC for prepared mushrooms used as closest parent; no specific code exists for mentaiko-topped crunchy mushroom dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific prepared mushroom dish with mentaiko sauce.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant portion of mentaiko crunchy mushroom, approximately 150 g, consisting of battered and fried mushrooms topped with mentaiko (spicy pollock roe) sauce.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Mushrooms, cooked, boiled, drained (SR Legacy 11265) combined with component estimation for battered fried mushrooms and mentaiko sauce
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for mentaiko crunchy mushroom exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis: battered deep-fried mushrooms (USDA SR Legacy 11265 as mushroom base, adjusted for frying absorption using USDA frying factor data) combined with mentaiko sauce contribution (spicy pollock roe and mayonnaise components, USDA FoodData Central NDB 15165 for pollock roe and NDB 04025 for mayonnaise). Energy, protein, fat, and carbohydrate values reflect the composite dish at approximately 60% mushroom, 20% batter/oil, 20% mentaiko sauce by weight. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — analogous estimate using battered/fried vegetable dishes and mushroom-based foods
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for mentaiko crunchy mushroom. GI estimated at 45 based on analogous battered fried vegetable dishes (GI range 40–55 per University of Sydney GI Database) and the low-GI contribution of mushrooms (GI ~15) combined with wheat batter (GI ~55). The mentaiko sauce (pollock roe and mayonnaise) contributes negligible carbohydrate. Weighted composite GI estimated at 45, classified as low (≤55). Glycemic load per 150 g serving = (45 × 15.5 g carbohydrate × 1.5) / 100 = 10.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from mentaiko (spicy pollock roe) and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its estimated sodium content of approximately 420 mg per 100 g (630 mg per 150 g serving), which represents a significant portion of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load from wheat batter with low estimated glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes may consume this dish in moderation; the estimated GI of 45 (low) and glycemic load of approximately 10.5 per serving are manageable, but the battered coating contributes refined carbohydrates and the high fat content from frying and mentaiko sauce warrants portion control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated fat content from deep-frying and mentaiko mayonnaise sauce, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish sparingly due to its estimated 11 g fat per 100 g (including 2.2 g saturated fat) from deep-frying and mentaiko sauce, as well as its high sodium content, both of which may adversely affect blood pressure and lipid profiles.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mentaiko Crunchy Mushroom is a Japanese-inspired dish consisting of mushrooms coated in a light wheat batter, deep-fried until golden and crispy, then topped or tossed with mentaiko sauce — a creamy, spicy condiment made from marinated pollock roe (tarako or karashi mentaiko) blended with mayonnaise and seasonings. The dish delivers a satisfying contrast of textures: the exterior is crunchy and golden from frying, while the mushroom interior remains tender and earthy. The mentaiko sauce adds a rich, umami-forward, mildly spicy flavour with a creamy finish. In Singapore, this dish is served at Japanese izakaya-style restaurants and sushi establishments such as GyoGyo, where it appears as a side dish or appetiser. Nutritionally, the dish is moderate in energy (approximately 185 kcal per 100 g) with meaningful protein from the pollock roe (approximately 6.5 g per 100 g), moderate fat from frying and sauce (approximately 11 g per 100 g), and carbohydrate from the batter (approximately 15.5 g per 100 g). Sodium content is elevated due to the cured roe and seasoning, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The estimated glycemic index is low (approximately 45), making it relatively suitable for blood glucose management when consumed in controlled portions. Mushrooms contribute dietary fibre, B vitamins (particularly riboflavin and niacin), and selenium. The dish is not suitable for individuals with fish, egg, gluten, or soy allergies.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for mentaiko crunchy mushroom was found in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central exact match, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis using USDA SR Legacy data for mushrooms, pollock roe, and mayonnaise, adjusted for frying. GI is an analogous estimate based on battered fried vegetable dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this estimation (USDA SR Legacy mushroom and pollock roe entries do not provide a reliable composite vitamin D figure for this preparation). All other -1 fields are not applicable or not reported in the source databases for this estimated composite dish. Nutrient values are estimates derived from component analysis and should be verified against laboratory analysis or a direct HPB Singapore Foodo entry if one becomes available. The food_group 'Vegetables' is preserved from the input record as mushrooms are the primary ingredient, though the dish also contains significant seafood-derived components. The restaurant identifier '704D' in the original food name appears to be a menu item code from GyoGyo - Funan and has been removed from the canonical food_name. GI of 45 is classified as low (≤55) per Veritas GI threshold rules.
